revision.text:
[[ja:温泉分析書]] [[File:Hot spring analyze documents.jpg|250px|thumb|Example of an Onsen Analysis Report
([[Kawayu Onsen Station]] footbath)]] '''Onsen Analysis Report''' (温泉分析書, Onsen Bunsekisho) is a document mandated by the [[Onsen Act]] (温泉法) to describe the components, contraindications, and usage guidelines for {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} (hot springs). == Overview == Under Article 18, Section 1 of the Onsen Act, onsen analysis reports must be displayed within onsen facilities, typically near dressing room entrances or inside the bathing areas. The reports are created by analysis agencies registered with the prefectural governor, as stipulated in Sections 2 and 4 of the same article. Additionally, the analysis details must be submitted to the respective prefectural governor. == Reanalysis Requirements == A 2007 amendment to the Onsen Act added requirements for periodic reanalysis of onsen properties every 10 years. The amendment, promulgated on April 25, 2007, and enforced on October 20, 2007, required all onsens to update their analysis reports by December 31, 2009. The governor may also mandate changes to the displayed content if deemed necessary to protect public health, according to Section 5 of Article 18. == Penalties for Non-Compliance == Violations of Sections 1–3 may result in fines of up to ¥300,000. Violations of directives under Section 5 may result in fines of up to ¥500,000. == Content == The Onsen Act Enforcement Regulations (温泉法施行規則), Article 10, outline the mandatory details to include in an analysis report: 1. Name of the source spring 2. Type of onsen water (quality classification) 3. Temperature of the water at the source and at the bathing or drinking site 4. Components of the water 5. Date of the most recent analysis 6. Name and registration number of the certified analysis agency 7. Contraindications for bathing or drinking 8. Recommended usage methods and precautions 9. Indication if water is added for public bathing purposes, along with reasons 10. Indication if water is heated for public bathing, along with reasons 11. Indication if the water is circulated for public bathing (including filtration, if applicable), along with reasons 12. Indication if additives (e.g., bath salts or disinfectants) are used, with their names or descriptions and reasons for usage == See Also == * {{ill|Onsen Fraud Scandal|ja|温泉偽装問題|1=Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=Onsen Fraud Scandal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Scandal|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Fraud Controversy|qq=}} * [[Guidelines for Mineral Spring Analysis]] (鉱泉分析法指針) == External Links == * {{Egov law|323AC0000000125|Onsen Act (温泉法)}} * [https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/pamph_aramashi.html Overview of Onsen Act Amendments - Ministry of the Environment (2007)] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|温泉分析書|version=104272623|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:温泉分析書]] {{wikidata link|Q11562932}} [[Category:Hot_springs_of_Japan]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
